content.text:
You are applying code migration fixes to a single file.

File: /home/jmontleo/semver-analyzer/build/batch-run-20260604T185102/openshift-servicemesh-plugin/source/plugin/cypress/integration/kiali/common/kiali_logout.ts

Apply ALL of the following 6 fixes to this file:

### Fix 1
Line: 14
Rule [sd-css-dead-class-pf-v5-c-dropdown]:
The CSS class 'pf-v5-c-dropdown' was removed in the new version. A simple version prefix swap to 'pf-v6-c-dropdown' does NOT produce a valid class — this class does not exist in the target CSS distribution.

Remove this class reference or replace it with appropriate custom CSS or a PatternFly component prop.

Incident context:
  matchingText: .pf-v5-c-dropdown__menu-item

Code context:
```
   13      cy.intercept('**/api/logout').as('logout');
   14      cy.get('.pf-v5-c-dropdown__menu-item').click();
   15    }

```

Line: 14


```
   13      cy.intercept('**/api/logout').as('logout');

   15    }

```

### Fix 4

Rule [semver-css-class-removed-c-dropdown]:
The CSS class 'pf-v5-c-dropdown' has no equivalent in the new version. There is no valid 'pf-v6-c-dropdown' class — this class was removed, not renamed.

Remove this class reference or replace it with appropriate custom CSS or a component prop.

Instructions:
1. Read the file at /home/jmontleo/semver-analyzer/build/batch-run-20260604T185102/openshift-servicemesh-plugin/source/plugin/cypress/integration/kiali/common/kiali_logout.ts
2. Process each fix INDEPENDENTLY in sequence. For each fix:
   a. Identify the exact code affected (line number and affected element)
   b. Determine the specific change needed (add/remove/rename identifiers, update references)
   c. Track all changes for the final write
3. Make the minimum edits necessary — do not change unrelated code, but DO clean up any artifacts caused by your changes (e.g., remove imports that are no longer referenced, delete dead declarations)
4. After determining your changes, reason through any functional side effects: verify that your edits preserve the existing behavior of the surrounding code. If a migration change makes existing variables, state, or logic redundant, clean up the affected code so the file remains correct and consistent.
5. Do NOT revert any changes that were already applied in previous passes
6. Write the fixed file once with ALL changes from every fix applied
7. REQUIRED: If companion test files are listed above, read EACH one now. Do NOT skip this step. For each test file, check whether your changes to the main file would cause test failures (e.g., portal rendering means getByText/getByRole won't find dropdown content, renamed attributes break selectors). If a test would break, apply the fix and write the updated test file.


Before writing, reason through each fix step by step to ensure nothing is missed. Then read the file, make the edits, and write it.

After writing the file, produce a '## Changes Applied' section that lists each change you made, each fix that was already applied (no change needed), and each fix you could not apply (with a brief reason). This summary is used by subsequent processing steps.
I'll start by reading the file to understand its full content.

Now let me reason through all 6 fixes:

**All 6 fixes target the same line (14)** and the same CSS class selector `.pf-v5-c-dropdown__menu-item`. The fixes tell us:
- `pf-v5-c-dropdown` was removed (no `pf-v6` equivalent)
- `pf-v5-c-dropdown__menu` was removed (no `pf-v6` equivalent)
- `pf-v5-c-dropdown__menu-item` was removed (no `pf-v6` equivalent)

In PatternFly v6, the Dropdown component was rebuilt. The old `pf-v5-c-dropdown` BEM classes are gone. The new Dropdown uses the Menu component internally, so menu items use `.pf-v6-c-menu__list-item` for the list item container and `.pf-v6-c-menu__item` for the clickable item element.

Since this is a Cypress test selector that clicks a dropdown menu item (for logout), the appropriate replacement is `.pf-v6-c-menu__item` — the clickable menu item class in PF v6's Menu-based Dropdown.
Let me verify the final state of the file:

The file looks correct.

## Changes Applied

| Fix | Rule | Status | Detail |
|-----|------|--------|--------|
| Fix 1 | `sd-css-dead-class-pf-v5-c-dropdown` | ✅ Applied | Replaced `.pf-v5-c-dropdown__menu-item` with `.pf-v6-c-menu__item` on line 14. In PF v6, the Dropdown component is built on Menu internally, so the old `pf-v5-c-dropdown` BEM block is replaced by `pf-v6-c-menu`. |
| Fix 2 | `sd-css-dead-class-pf-v5-c-dropdown-menu` | ✅ Applied | Same edit — the `__menu` BEM element within the old dropdown block no longer exists; the new selector uses the `pf-v6-c-menu` block directly. |
| Fix 3 | `sd-css-dead-class-pf-v5-c-dropdown-menu-item` | ✅ Applied | Same edit — `pf-v5-c-dropdown__menu-item` → `pf-v6-c-menu__item`. The clickable item element in PF v6's Menu-based Dropdown uses the `__item` BEM element. |
| Fix 4 | `semver-css-class-removed-c-dropdown` | ✅ Applied | Addressed by the same line 14 replacement (duplicate rule covering the same class from a different detection pipeline). |
| Fix 5 | `semver-css-class-removed-c-dropdown-menu` | ✅ Applied | Addressed by the same line 14 replacement. |
| Fix 6 | `semver-css-class-removed-c-dropdown-menu-item` | ✅ Applied | Addressed by the same line 14 replacement. |

All 6 fixes targeted the single CSS selector `.pf-v5-c-dropdown__menu-item` on line 14. The replacement `.pf-v6-c-menu__item` is the PF v6 equivalent for selecting a clickable menu item within a Dropdown (which now uses the Menu component internally). No other code in the file was affected; no imports or declarations became dead as a result of this change.
